name: Winter Squash Soup
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: Better Homes and Gardens - Soups and Stews
Preparation Time: 0:45
1. Halve the squash; remove seeds.
2. Cut each piece in half.
3. Place a steamer basket in a large saucepan or Dutch oven; add broth to the saucepan. Bring to boiling; place squash in the steamer basket.
4. Steam for 25 to 30 minutes, or until squash can be pierced easily with a fork. Carefully remove steamer basket from the saucepan.
5. Reserve the steaming liquid in the saucepan.
6. Using a spoon, scoop squash pulp out of the peel; discard the peel.
7. Place squash pulp in a blender or food processor. Cover and process until the mixture is smooth, adding as much of the reserved liquid as necessary.
8. Stir pureed squash into the remaining reserved liquid in the saucepan; stir in ginger, salt, pepper, and cinnamon. Bring to boiling.
9. Reduce heat and stir in half-and-half. Heat through.
10. If desired, garnish each serving with a dollop of sour cream and chives.
